all.en: Mom had been planning Dad's fiftieth birthday party in secret for two whole months.
all.zh: 媽媽已經偷偷策劃爸爸五十歲的生日派對整整兩個月了。

all.en: She invited his colleagues, college friends, and relatives from all over the country.
all.zh: 她邀請了他的同事、大學朋友，還有來自全國各地的親戚。

all.en: The hardest part was keeping the kids quiet about the whole thing.
all.zh: 最難的部分是要孩子們保密這整件事。

all.en: Twelve-year-old Daniel almost let it slip during breakfast on Saturday morning.
all.zh: 十二歲的丹尼爾星期六早上吃早餐時差點說溜了嘴。

all.en: Luckily, Mom quickly changed the subject and gave him a warning look.
all.zh: 還好媽媽很快轉移話題，並給了他一個警告的眼神。

all.en: While Dad was at the gym, twenty guests quietly arrived and hid in the living room.
all.zh: 爸爸去健身房時，二十位客人悄悄抵達並躲在客廳裡。

all.en: Daniel was assigned to greet Dad at the door and bring him inside.
all.zh: 丹尼爾被指派在門口迎接爸爸，並把他帶進屋裡。

all.en: When Dad opened the door, the lights flashed on and everyone shouted "Surprise!"
all.zh: 當爸爸打開門時，燈一下子全亮了，所有人大喊「驚喜！」

all.en: Dad froze for a moment, then his eyes filled with tears.
all.zh: 爸爸愣了一下，然後眼睛盈滿淚水。

all.en: His best friend from college had flown all the way from Canada just for this.
all.zh: 他大學最要好的朋友特地從加拿大飛來參加這場派對。

all.en: Mom presented a huge chocolate cake with fifty candles, which took ages to blow out.
all.zh: 媽媽端出一個有五十根蠟燭的大巧克力蛋糕，吹了好久才吹熄。

all.en: Dad hugged Mom tightly and whispered that this was the best birthday of his life.
all.zh: 爸爸緊緊抱住媽媽，輕聲說這是他這輩子最棒的生日。

all.en: After everyone had eaten, Dad's college friend stood up and gave a touching speech.
all.zh: 大家用餐後，爸爸的大學朋友站起來發表了一段感人的致詞。

all.en: He told funny stories from their student days that even Mom had never heard before.
all.zh: 他分享了一些連媽媽都沒聽過的學生時代趣事。

all.en: Dad laughed until his face turned red and his eyes watered all over again.
all.zh: 爸爸笑到臉都紅了，眼淚又一次湧了出來。

all.en: Daniel surprised everyone by performing a guitar song he had secretly learned for the party.
all.zh: 丹尼爾讓所有人驚訝的是，他演奏了一首為派對偷偷練習的吉他曲。

all.en: Dad couldn't believe his son had practiced so hard without making a single noise at home.
all.zh: 爸爸不敢相信兒子能在家裡完全不出聲地練到這麼熟。

all.en: Mom presented a photo album she had collected from friends and relatives across the years.
all.zh: 媽媽拿出一本她多年來向親友收集照片做成的相簿。

all.en: Dad flipped through the pages slowly, recognizing faces from every chapter of his life.
all.zh: 爸爸慢慢翻著相簿，認出自己人生每個階段的面孔。

all.en: The party lasted past midnight, and the guests sang old songs around the piano.
all.zh: 派對一直到深夜才結束，客人們圍著鋼琴唱起了懷舊老歌。

all.en: When the last guest left, Dad sat on the sofa and quietly thanked his wife again.
all.zh: 最後一位客人離開後，爸爸坐在沙發上再次輕聲向妻子道謝。

all.en: Mom smiled and said his happiness was the only birthday gift she had really wanted.
all.zh: 媽媽微笑說，他的快樂是她真正想要的唯一生日禮物。

all.en: The morning after the party, Dad woke up late and found the house still decorated with balloons.
all.zh: 派對隔天早上，爸爸睡晚了，發現家裡還佈滿著氣球。

all.en: He laughed when he saw Daniel snoring on the sofa with the guitar still in his arms.
all.zh: 他看到丹尼爾在沙發上打呼，吉他還抱在懷裡，忍不住笑了出來。

all.en: Mom prepared a slow breakfast of pancakes, fresh fruit, and Dad's favorite black coffee.
all.zh: 媽媽悠閒地準備了早餐，有鬆餅、新鮮水果，還有爸爸最愛的黑咖啡。

all.en: During the meal, Dad opened a few cards from relatives who could not attend the night before.
all.zh: 用餐時，爸爸拆開幾張來自前晚無法出席的親戚寄來的卡片。

all.en: One card from his uncle contained an old photo of Dad as a five-year-old on a tricycle.
all.zh: 叔叔寄來的一張卡片裡，夾著一張爸爸五歲騎三輪車的舊照片。

all.en: Dad showed the photo to Daniel, who said he had never seen Dad with so much hair before.
all.zh: 爸爸把照片拿給丹尼爾看，丹尼爾說他從沒看過爸爸頭髮這麼茂密的樣子。

all.en: Everyone laughed loudly, and Dad pretended to be offended for only about three seconds.
all.zh: 大家笑得不可開交，爸爸只裝生氣了大約三秒鐘。

all.en: Later, Dad called his college friend to thank him again for flying all the way from Canada.
all.zh: 稍晚，爸爸打電話給大學朋友，再次感謝他特地從加拿大飛來。

all.en: They promised each other to meet again next summer, this time on the beach in Kenting.
all.zh: 他們約好明年夏天再聚，這次要去墾丁的海邊。

all.en: Dad ended the day by writing a long thank-you message to every single guest in his phone.
all.zh: 爸爸在這天的尾聲，用手機寫了一封長長的感謝訊息給每一位賓客。

all.en: A week after the party, Dad received a handmade scrapbook from his colleagues at the office.
all.zh: 派對過後一週，爸爸收到了同事們親手做的剪貼簿。

all.en: It contained funny captions, group photos, and silly drawings from every department in the company.
all.zh: 裡面有幽默的圖說、合照，還有公司每個部門畫的搞笑塗鴉。

all.en: Dad displayed the scrapbook on the coffee table so visitors could enjoy it whenever they came over.
all.zh: 爸爸把剪貼簿擺在咖啡桌上，這樣訪客來時隨時都能翻閱。

all.en: Mom suggested they host a small thank-you brunch for the closest friends who had helped her plan.
all.zh: 媽媽提議辦一場小型感謝早午餐，邀請當初幫忙策劃的好朋友們。

all.en: Daniel volunteered to play a longer guitar piece, this time without keeping it a secret.
all.zh: 丹尼爾自願再表演一段更長的吉他曲，這次不用偷偷練習了。

all.en: On the day of the brunch, sunlight streamed through the windows and filled the room with warmth.
all.zh: 早午餐當天，陽光從窗戶灑進來，整個房間都充滿了暖意。

all.en: Dad gave a short speech, thanking each guest by name and recalling a memory they had shared.
all.zh: 爸爸發表了一段簡短致詞，逐一感謝每位賓客，並分享一段與對方相關的回憶。

all.en: Several guests wiped their eyes, surprised that Dad could remember such small but precious details.
all.zh: 幾位賓客擦著眼淚，驚訝爸爸竟能記得這麼細微卻珍貴的小事。

all.en: After the brunch, Daniel hugged his father and admitted he hoped his own fiftieth would be just as warm.
all.zh: 早午餐結束後，丹尼爾擁抱爸爸，坦言希望自己五十歲時也能這麼溫暖。

all.en: Dad smiled and promised that the family would always make space for these meaningful celebrations.
all.zh: 爸爸微笑著承諾，這個家永遠會為這些有意義的慶祝活動騰出空間。

all.en: Months later, Dad surprised the family by announcing he wanted to plan a similar party for Mom.
all.zh: 幾個月後，爸爸出其不意地宣布他要為媽媽策劃一場類似的派對。

all.en: Daniel volunteered to handle the secret guest list, having learned from Mom's careful methods.
all.zh: 丹尼爾自願負責保密的賓客名單，他已從媽媽那細心的方式學到不少。

all.en: They reserved the small backyard garden, knowing how much Mom loved being surrounded by flowers.
all.zh: 他們訂下了小小的後院花園，因為他們知道媽媽多麼喜歡被花朵環繞。

all.en: Dad ordered a custom cake shaped like Mom's favorite flower, the white camellia in full bloom.
all.zh: 爸爸訂了一個客製化蛋糕，造型是媽媽最愛的花，盛開的白茶花。

all.en: Daniel composed a brand new guitar song especially for Mom and practiced it at his friend's house.
all.zh: 丹尼爾為媽媽特地譜寫了一首全新的吉他曲，並在朋友家偷偷練習。

all.en: On the morning of the party, Mom was sent to a fake hair appointment downtown to keep her busy.
all.zh: 派對當天早上，媽媽被「派」去市區一個假的美髮預約，藉此分散她的注意力。

all.en: When she finally returned, the garden glowed with lanterns and twenty smiling guests waited eagerly.
all.zh: 當她終於回家時，花園裡掛滿了燈籠，二十位帶著笑容的賓客熱切地等候著。

all.en: Mom burst into laughter and tears at the same time, hugging Dad and Daniel tightly in turn.
all.zh: 媽媽又笑又哭，輪流緊緊地擁抱了爸爸和丹尼爾。

all.en: Daniel performed his new song under the lanterns while everyone listened in respectful silence.
all.zh: 丹尼爾在燈籠下演奏他的新曲，所有人都靜靜地、帶著敬意聆聽著。

all.en: Mom whispered that she finally understood the joy Dad had felt on his own surprise day.
all.zh: 媽媽悄聲說，她終於體會到爸爸在自己驚喜派對那天所感受到的那份喜悅。

all.en: After Mom's surprise party, the family decided to make secret celebrations a quiet new tradition.
all.zh: 媽媽的驚喜派對之後，全家決定把秘密慶祝變成一個悄悄展開的新傳統。

all.en: Daniel began composing a song for each upcoming birthday, hiding the lyrics until the very last moment.
all.zh: 丹尼爾開始為每個即將到來的生日創作一首歌，把歌詞藏到最後一刻才公開。

all.en: Dad volunteered to handle decorations, perfecting his skill at hanging lanterns along every garden branch.
all.zh: 爸爸自願負責佈置，把在花園枝椏間掛燈籠的功夫練到爐火純青。

all.en: Mom researched new recipes monthly, surprising the family with international dishes from places she dreamed of visiting.
all.zh: 媽媽每個月研究新食譜，用她夢想造訪之地的異國料理給全家驚喜。

all.en: For Daniel's seventeenth birthday, Mom and Dad invited his entire band to play a backyard concert at sunset.
all.zh: 丹尼爾十七歲生日時，爸媽邀請了他整個樂團在後院舉辦黃昏演唱會。

all.en: Daniel was speechless when his bandmates appeared one by one, wearing matching shirts Mom had secretly ordered.
all.zh: 看到團員們一個接一個出現、穿著媽媽偷偷訂製的同款襯衫時，丹尼爾驚訝得說不出話。

all.en: The concert lasted late into the night, and even the neighbors brought out chairs to enjoy the music.
all.zh: 演唱會一直持續到深夜，連鄰居都搬出椅子來一起欣賞音樂。

all.en: Dad took dozens of photos, capturing every smile and every glittering lantern in the warm garden air.
all.zh: 爸爸拍了好幾十張照片，把溫暖花園空氣中的每個笑容和每盞閃爍燈籠都記錄下來。

all.en: Mom whispered that the family had discovered something more valuable than gifts, the joy of giving each other presence.
all.zh: 媽媽輕聲說，全家發現了比禮物更珍貴的東西，就是彼此陪伴的喜悅。

all.en: Before bed, Daniel promised his parents that the next surprise party would be entirely his to organize.
all.zh: 睡前丹尼爾向爸媽承諾，下一場驚喜派對由他全權負責規劃。

all.en: Daniel began researching ideas for his parents' upcoming wedding anniversary several months in advance.
all.zh: 丹尼爾提前好幾個月就開始為爸媽即將到來的結婚紀念日構思點子。

all.en: He secretly contacted the small restaurant where his parents had eaten on their very first date together.
all.zh: 他偷偷聯絡爸媽第一次約會時光顧的那間小餐廳。

all.en: The owner was deeply touched and offered to reserve the same corner table they had sat at decades ago.
all.zh: 老闆深受感動，主動為他們保留了幾十年前他們坐過的同一張角落桌。

all.en: Daniel created a custom playlist of songs that had played on the radio during their college years.
all.zh: 丹尼爾製作了一份客製歌單，全是他們大學時代廣播裡播過的歌。

all.en: He coordinated with relatives who recorded short video messages from all over the country.
all.zh: 他與全國各地的親戚協調，蒐集了一段段短短的影片祝福。

all.en: On the anniversary evening, he led his parents into the restaurant under the pretext of a family meeting.
all.zh: 紀念日當晚，他以家庭聚會為藉口，把爸媽帶到那間餐廳。

all.en: Mom recognized the restaurant immediately and grabbed Dad's hand with tears welling up in her eyes.
all.zh: 媽媽一眼就認出這間餐廳，眼眶泛淚地握住爸爸的手。

all.en: Daniel played the video compilation on a small projector, and every guest in the restaurant fell quiet.
all.zh: 丹尼爾用小型投影機播放剪輯影片，整間餐廳裡的客人都靜了下來。

all.en: Dad whispered that their son had inherited his mother's gift for turning ordinary days into lifelong memories.
all.zh: 爸爸悄聲說，他們的兒子繼承了媽媽把平凡日子化作畢生回憶的天賦。

all.en: That night, the family agreed that the tradition of surprises had quietly become their truest love language.
all.zh: 那晚，全家一致認為，驚喜的傳統悄悄成為了他們最真摯的愛之語言。

all.en: Months after the anniversary dinner, Daniel created a small shared online album for every family celebration.
all.zh: 紀念日晚餐過後幾個月，丹尼爾建立了一個小型線上共享相簿，用來收錄每一場家庭慶祝。

all.en: Mom helped organize each event by date, adding short captions to remind everyone of the small details.
all.zh: 媽媽協助按日期整理每場活動，並加上簡短說明，提醒大家那些容易遺忘的小細節。

all.en: Dad printed a yearbook from the album every December and gave one copy to each close relative.
all.zh: 爸爸每年十二月都會把相簿印成一本年度紀念冊，送一本給每位親近的親戚。

all.en: Daniel's college friends began asking for tips on how to plan thoughtful surprises for their own parents.
all.zh: 丹尼爾的大學朋友開始向他請教如何為自己的父母策劃用心的驚喜。

all.en: He started a small blog sharing free planning checklists, simple guest list templates, and decoration tutorials.
all.zh: 他開了一個小型部落格，分享免費的籌備清單、簡易賓客名單範本和佈置教學。

all.en: Within a year, the blog had attracted readers from many cities, all hoping to celebrate their loved ones better.
all.zh: 不到一年，這個部落格吸引了來自許多城市的讀者，他們都希望能為親愛的人辦更好的慶祝。

all.en: Mom volunteered to test recipes for the blog, photographing each finished dish under the warm kitchen light.
all.zh: 媽媽自願幫部落格試做食譜，在溫暖的廚房燈光下為每道完成的菜拍照。

all.en: Dad contributed a section on backyard lighting, sharing safety tips and beautiful lantern arrangement ideas.
all.zh: 爸爸貢獻了一個後院燈光的專欄，分享安全要點和美麗的燈籠擺設構想。

all.en: A small publisher noticed the blog and offered Daniel a contract to write a short illustrated book.
all.zh: 一家小型出版社注意到這個部落格，向丹尼爾提出合約，邀請他撰寫一本短篇的插圖書。

all.en: Daniel accepted, knowing that one secret birthday party had quietly grown into a lifelong family gift to others.
all.zh: 丹尼爾接下了合約，明白那場秘密生日派對悄悄成長為一份家族能傳遞給他人的終身禮物。

all.en: On the day his publisher contract arrived, Daniel spilled his coffee on the envelope and panicked for almost five minutes before realizing the document was unharmed.
all.zh: 出版合約寄到那天，丹尼爾把咖啡灑在信封上，慌張了快五分鐘，才發現裡面的文件其實沒事。

all.en: Mom hosted a small celebration brunch and surprised him with a cake shaped like an open book with edible page corners.
all.zh: 媽媽辦了一頓小型慶祝早午餐，端出一個書本造型的蛋糕，連書頁角落都可以吃。

all.en: Dad volunteered to handle all the lanterns for the book launch event, calling it the perfect excuse to buy newer LED strings.
all.zh: 爸爸自願包辦新書發表會的所有燈籠，說這正是換新 LED 燈串的絕佳藉口。

all.en: Daniel asked his old elementary school teacher to write the dedication page, since she had taught him his first cursive handwriting strokes.
all.zh: 丹尼爾邀請小學時的老師寫獻詞頁，因為當年是她教他寫第一筆連筆字。

all.en: The teacher wept when she read the early manuscript, recognizing several childhood scenes in the warm illustrated chapters.
all.zh: 老師讀到初稿時哭了出來，因為在那些溫暖的插圖章節裡認出了好幾個童年場景。

all.en: On launch day, Daniel set up the venue using lanterns from every family celebration the book described.
all.zh: 新書發表當天，丹尼爾把書中每一場家族慶祝出現過的燈籠都搬出來佈置會場。

all.en: An older couple bought twelve copies, one for each grandchild scattered across three different cities.
all.zh: 一對年長夫妻買了十二本，要送給散居在三個不同城市的每位孫子孫女。

all.en: Daniel handwrote a short personal note inside each copy, even though it took him three full evenings to finish them all.
all.zh: 丹尼爾在每本書裡都親手寫了一段短短的留言，雖然花了他整整三個晚上才寫完。

all.en: Mom quietly slipped the original birthday party invitation into a glass display case beside the bookshop's counter.
all.zh: 媽媽默默把當年那張生日派對邀請函放進書店櫃台旁的玻璃展示盒裡。

all.en: Before turning off the lights that night, Daniel sat alone in the empty bookshop and whispered a quiet thank-you to every secret his family had ever kept.
all.zh: 那晚關燈前，丹尼爾獨自坐在空蕩蕩的書店裡，對著家裡曾守過的每個秘密輕聲說了句謝謝。

all.en: We hid all the decorations in the garage so Dad wouldn't notice.
all.zh: 我們把所有裝飾品都藏在車庫裡，免得爸爸發現。

all.en: Mom baked his favorite chocolate cake the night before the party.
all.zh: 派對前一晚，媽媽烤了他最愛的巧克力蛋糕。

all.en: My job was to keep Dad out of the house until six o'clock.
all.zh: 我的任務是把爸爸支開，直到六點才能回家。

all.en: Everyone crouched behind the sofa, trying not to giggle.
all.zh: 大家都蹲在沙發後面，努力忍住不笑出來。

all.en: The moment he opened the door, we all shouted "Surprise!"
all.zh: 他一打開門，我們就齊聲大喊「驚喜！」

all.en: Dad was so touched that his eyes filled with happy tears.
all.zh: 爸爸非常感動，眼裡盈滿了喜悅的淚水。

all.en: Old friends he hadn't seen in years showed up to celebrate.
all.zh: 好幾位多年不見的老朋友都來慶祝了。

all.en: We played music and danced in the living room until midnight.
all.zh: 我們在客廳裡放音樂跳舞，一直到半夜。

all.en: He kept saying it was the best birthday of his entire life.
all.zh: 他一直說這是他這輩子最棒的生日。

all.en: Keeping the secret for two months was totally worth it in the end.
all.zh: 把祕密保守兩個月，最後證明完全值得。
